explanation: The property states that globally, whenever an initialization failure occurs, the system must simultaneously issue an out-of-service warning and ensure that no other normal operations are allowed. 'Other operations' are defined as a disjunction of common operational modes and user-initiated actions that constitute the system's normal functionality.
bool_exp3: (OutOfServiceWarning & !(ventilating | PSVMode | PCVMode | RM | inspiratoryPause | expiratoryPause | startMonitoring | startReportingHealthParams | saveLog | loadLog | eraseLog | enableLeakCompensation | confirmPSVParameters | patientAttributesEntered | newPatient | patientChanged | enterAlarmThresholds | ventilatorSettingsChanged | alarmSettingsChanged | resumeVentilation))

explanation: The property states that globally, if an initialization failure occurs, then at that moment, the user shall be warned that the system is out-of-service, and additionally, the system shall enter a fail-safe mode, which implies that any other operations are not allowed. This captures both parts of the natural language statement: the warning (OutOfServiceWarning) and the cessation of normal operations (represented by entering failSafeMode).

The property states that globally (G), whenever an initialization failure (initFail) occurs, the system must issue an out-of-service warning (OutOfServiceWarning) and simultaneously ensure that core operational activities such as ventilation (ventilating), pressure support ventilation mode (PSVMode), pressure controlled ventilation mode (PCVMode), and recruitment maneuver (RM) are not allowed. This directly translates the natural language requirement that upon an initialization failure, the user is warned the system is out-of-service, and no other operations are permitted.

The property states that globally, whenever an initialization failure occurs (represented by 'initFail'), the system must simultaneously indicate that it is out-of-service ('OutOfServiceWarning') and transition into a fail-safe mode ('failSafeMode'). This captures the requirement that upon detection of an initialization failure, the user is warned, and all other operations are disallowed as the system enters a restricted state.

The property states that globally (G), if an initialization failure (bool_exp1: initFail) occurs, then eventually (F) the system will reach a state where an out-of-service warning is displayed (OutOfServiceWarning) AND any core operational activities (bool_exp3: OutOfServiceWarning & !(ventilating | PSVMode | PCVMode | RM | inspiratoryPause | expiratoryPause)) are not allowed. This captures both the explicit warning and the implied cessation of normal operations as a consequence of the failure.
bool_exp3: OutOfServiceWarning & !(ventilating | PSVMode | PCVMode | RM | inspiratoryPause | expiratoryPause)

explanation: The property states that globally, if an initialization failure occurs (initFail), then the system shall always remain in an out-of-service warning state (OutOfServiceWarning). This captures both that the user is warned and that, by being 'out-of-service', other operations are inherently not allowed.

The property states that globally, if an initialization failure (initFail) occurs, then the system must display an out-of-service warning (OutOfServiceWarning). The phrase 'any other operations shall be not allowed' is considered implicitly covered by the system being in an 'out-of-service' state, as indicated by the 'OutOfServiceWarning' atomic proposition.

This property states that globally, if an initialization failure occurs (bool_exp1), then the system shall simultaneously issue an out-of-service warning and prevent the ventilation process from occurring (bool_exp3). This captures both conditions specified in the natural language: the warning and the prohibition of further operations (represented by ventilation).

The property states that globally, if an initialization failure occurs, it must always lead to two conditions being met simultaneously: the system displaying an out-of-service warning and preventing any normal operational modes (ventilation, PSV, PCV, or standby). 'bool_exp1' captures the 'initialization failure' as the antecedent, and 'bool_exp3' captures the conjoined consequences: 'OutOfServiceWarning' for the user warning and a negation of common operational modes (ventilating, PSVMode, PCVMode, StandbyMode) for 'any other operations shall be not allowed'.

The property states that globally (G), if an initialization failure (initFail) occurs, then it must always be true that the system issues an out-of-service warning (OutOfServiceWarning) AND that core operational states and actions (ventilating, PSVMode, PCVMode, resumeVentilation, startMonitoring, startReportingHealthParams) are not active. This captures both requirements: warning the user and disallowing other operations upon an initialization failure.
